EX PARTE APPLICATION ON TRO HEARING IS HELD. COUNSEL: ERIC VANDEVELDE FOR PETITIONER, JOHN PERNICK FOR RESPONDENT PRESENT VIA COURT CALL. MERCURY TECHNOLOGIES INC.S EX PARTE APPLLCATION FOR A TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. WHILE MERCURY SEEKS IMMEDIATE EXTRAORDINARY RELIEF, THE MOST RECENT EVENTS DATED IN ITS MEMORANDUM OCCURRED BACK IN OCTOBER. MERCURY MAY FILE FOR A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IN A REGULAR NOTICED MOTION. THIS WOULD PERMIT THE RESPONDING PARTY TO BE HEARD FULLY AND ALLOW REASONED CONSIDERATION BY THE COURT. NO FORMAL ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.,CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU

Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Off Calendar

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR# 10619 (tel: 408-390-6445 / e-mail: raquel@transcriptlegal.com)(302/RBU)

Motion And Renewed Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Mar-05-2024 Continued To Apr-30-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER MERCURY TECHNOLOGIES INC.'s Motion And Renewed Motion To Seal.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

On January 24, 2024, this court denied applicant Mercury Technologies, Inc.'s petition for writ of attachment and its motion to seal. The latter denial was without prejudice, so Mercury has filed a "renewed" sealing motion. The hearing on that motion is continued to April 30, 2024.

Mercury and respondent Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc. shall do the following:

Mercury shall create (1) a tabbed binder that provides each document it seeks to seal any part of (the unredacted version immediately followed by the redacted) and (2) a table like the one in Mercury's proposed order that (a) identifies (by name and reference to the tabbed binder) the document it wants sealed, (b) the pages and line numbers proposed for sealing, (c) the ground(s) for sealing, including citation to legal authority and (d) a fourth column in which Synapse shall provide its ground(s) for not sealing, including citation to legal authority, or an indication that it does not oppose the particular sealing. (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894; CRC 2.550.)

Mercury shall perform its work by March 25 and Synapse its work by April 15, at which time Mercury shall lodge the final work product with the judge for his review in camera.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order, Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Jan-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. ERIC D. VANDEVELDE, Esq. of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (Tel: 213-229-7000) for petitioner Mercury Technologies Inc. (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. JOHN D. PERNICK, Esq. of Bergeson, LLP (Tel: 408-291-2253) for respondent Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MERCURY TECHNOLOGIES INC.'s Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order, Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Mercury Technologies Inc.'s petition for writ of attachment is denied.

Parties to arbitration proceedings may apply to the superior court for writs of attachment if, among other requirements, an award to the petitioner "may be rendered ineffectual" without such relief. (CCP 1281.8.) Apparent insolvency or other evidence of severe financial difficulties can satisfy the ineffectual relief requirement. (California Retail Portfolio Fund GMBH & Co. KG v. Hopkins Real Estate Group (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 849.) This can be inferred from the defendant's failure to timely pay debts not subject to a bona fide dispute. (Id. at 858.) Beyond this minimum requirement, the party seeking injunctive relief must also demonstrate it is likely to prevail on the merits, has no adequate alternative remedy, and will suffer irreparable harm if the injunction is denied. (Davenport v. Blue Cross of California (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 435, 450.)

Mercury argues relief will be ineffectual based on respondent Synapse Financial Technologies' apparent insolvency or severe financial difficulties. But Mercury has not shown Synapse is failing to pay undisputed debts. Synapse disputes amounts allegedly owed to Mercury and others. (Pathak Decl. para. 4-6.) To the extent Synapse acknowledges proportionally small amounts of undisputed debts, it avers those debts have or will be paid. (Pathak Decl. para. 4, 6.) Mercury has not shown on this record that Synapse is generally not paying undisputed debts. Because Mercury cannot make the threshold showing of ineffectual relief, its petition is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR No. 12998, Email: karla@sf-reporters.com, Tel: 415-948-8289 (302/RBU)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Jan-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. ERIC D. VANDEVELDE, Esq. of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (Tel: 213-229-7000) for petitioner Mercury Technologies Inc. (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. JOHN D. PERNICK, Esq. of Bergeson, LLP (Tel: 408-291-2253) for respondent Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MERCURY TECHNOLOGIES INC.'s Motion To Seal.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Mercury Technologies Inc.'s motion to seal is denied without prejudice.

To enable a court to make the specific findings required by California Rule of Court 2.550(d), a party moving to seal court records must present "specific enumeration of the facts sought to be withheld and specific reasons for withholding them." (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) Here, while portions of documents are laundry-listed, the specific facts sought to be withheld are not enumerated. Moreover, while broad reasons for withholding documents (e.g., competitors would "gain a competitive advantage") are also laundry-listed, little or no attempt is made to link facts with reasons.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR No. 12998, Email: karla@sf-reporters.com, Tel: 415-948-8289 (302/RBU)
